title: PacBio Announces Second Quarter 2023 Financial Results
text: PacBio registered Q2 2023 revenue of $47.6 million, up 34.1% year-over-year from $35.5 million, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 17.9%. Product revenue was $43.7 million, consumables revenue was $13.7 million, and service and other revenue was $3.9 million. Gross profit was $15.5 million with a 33% margin, down from 46% prior year. Net loss was $69.8 million, improved from $71.4 million loss in Q2 2022. Net loss per share was $0.28 versus $0.32 prior year. Adjusted loss per share was 26 cents, narrower than the Zacks Consensus Estimate of 34 cents per share. 47 sequencing systems were recognized for revenue: 45 Revio and 2 Sequel IIe systems. Instrument revenue nearly doubled to $29.9 million from $15.6 million. Cash and investments reached $829.9 million with total assets of $1.85 billion. CEO Christian Henry noted Revio demand drove record quarterly revenue as manufacturing scaled and customers began ramp sequencing on Revio. The company commenced Onso platform shipments and agreed to acquire Apton Biosystems to develop high-throughput short-read sequencing capabilities. The rapid adoption of Revio drove another record quarter for PacBio with 34% revenue growth compared to Q2 prior year. They exceeded $40 million in quarterly revenue for the first time in history with each region posting record revenue. Revio momentum increased confidence to raise 2023 revenue expectations to between $185 million and $190 million, representing 44% to 48% growth over 2022.
